frameworks.name: decoded fMRI neurofeedback
frameworks.one_line: real-time multivoxel decoding used to drive self-regulation of pain-related brain patterns

frameworks.name: multivariate brain decoders (SIIPS, NPS)
frameworks.one_line: pre-trained pain signatures repurposed as targets in closed-loop neurofeedback

frameworks.name: causal brain-behavior mapping
frameworks.one_line: testing whether modulating decoded patterns causally alters subjective pain

core_question: Can decoded fMRI neurofeedback targeting pain-related multivoxel patterns causally modulate subjective pain perception?
key_assumptions: SIIPS and NPS capture dissociable components of pain processing
decoded pattern expression can be modulated via voluntary self-regulation
real-time MNI-space decoding is accurate enough for closed-loop training
manipulations: direction of neurofeedback regulation (up- vs down-regulation of SIIPS)
decoded signature targeted (SIIPS independent of NPS)
key_findings: subjective pain ratings predicted accurately in real time using SIIPS and NPS
participants learned to down-regulate SIIPS expression independently of NPS
neurofeedback success was associated with reduced perceived pain intensity post-intervention
interpretations: SIIPS modulation has a causal contribution to subjective pain experience
decoded fMRI neurofeedback is a viable causal probe and potential pain intervention
